name: Medication Conflict Patient
description: Patient with a documented allergy to Penicillin who is being prescribed Amoxicillin (a penicillin-class antibiotic) at discharge. System must detect this life-threatening allergy conflict and block summary generation.

admission.chief_complaint: Productive cough, fever, pleuritic chest pain

allergies.documented_in: Allergy band, admission note, MAR header

medications_discharge.name: Amoxicillin
medications_discharge.note: CRITICAL ALERT: Patient has documented life-threatening Penicillin allergy. Amoxicillin is a penicillin-class antibiotic.

conflicts.description: Amoxicillin (penicillin-class) prescribed at discharge despite documented life-threatening anaphylaxis to Penicillin (2019 ICU admission).

clinical_context.hospital_course: 44-year-old female admitted with community-acquired pneumonia. Treated with IV Azithromycin with good clinical response. Transitioned to oral antibiotics at discharge.

blocking_issues: Amoxicillin prescribed despite life-threatening Penicillin allergy

test_purpose: THE most critical safety test. Validates that the allergy-medication conflict detection is functioning correctly and that the system NEVER generates a summary with a life-threatening medication-allergy conflict present.
